title: Funding rate — why we read it before every signal
_note: EVERGREEN v2 (#CryptoSchool). Retoned to Variant B (data-check edge). Numeric example replaced with NEUTRAL wording (no invented numbers); CTA via {{COMMUNITY}} marker (dropped if COMMUNITY_LINK empty). No placeholders → ready.
body:
🎓 Funding rate — and why we read it before every signal

What it is
On perpetual futures, funding is a small periodic payment passed directly between longs and shorts. It's what keeps the perp price tethered to spot.

Why we care (and you should)
Funding tells you which side is crowded. Strongly positive = longs are paying shorts — everyone's leaning long, and you're paying to sit there. Strongly negative = the opposite. At extremes, the crowded side is exactly the side that gets liquidated first.

How we use it
Before any signal ships, we pull funding straight off Bitunix (aggregators don't cover it) and overlay the liquidation heatmap. Two things come out of that:
• Extreme funding → the crowd is offside → we lean toward fading, not following.
• We place the SL AWAY from the liquidation magnet, not on top of it — so a stop-hunt wick doesn't take us out before the idea plays.

Quick example
When funding is positive and you hold a long, you pay a small amount to the shorts at each funding interval. Tiny per interval — but at 50-100x it compounds fast, and the exact figure depends on the live funding rate and your position size. Check it on the exchange and price it in before you enter.

This is the kind of data-check behind every #Signal we post. Questions about funding? Ask in the community — that's what it's for. 👉 {{COMMUNITY}}

#CryptoSchool
